summary: rc.26 Doctor i18n Closure — Close the long-standing gap between KT-DEC-9004 (fabric_language as authoritative locale source) and runtime: `fab doctor` output remains English even when user has set `fabric_language: zh-CN` in .fabric/fabric-config.json. Three axes: (A) new `resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot)` shared helper that reads fabric_language with detectNodeLocale fallback; (B) migrate all 84 `okCheck()` / `issueCheck()` sites in packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts to translator-driven keys; (C) wire `packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts` to consume the projectRoot-aware translator via a `getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot)` factory (γ pattern — zero churn on non-doctor CLI commands). Plural handling via explicit `.singular`/`.plural` key pairs (no ICU parser extension). Locale strings: ~250 keys per locale (en + zh-CN). Acceptance: bilingual output verified by snapshot test + dogfood evidence in pcf repo.
approach: 6-task chain across 4 waves. Wave 1 (foundation): resolveFabricLocale helper + helper unit test + switch serve-lock.ts + reshape cli/src/i18n.ts to factory (γ pattern). Wave 2 (doctor migration, sequential — same-file collision): three batches by line range — TASK-02 bootstrap/foundation (L2087-3034, ~26 sites), TASK-03 knowledge lint (L3793-4697, ~29 sites), TASK-04 skill/onboard/cite (L4836-5170, ~29 sites). Each task commits independently after pnpm test. Wave 3 (locale completion + CLI rewire): TASK-05 backfills all keys in en.ts + zh-CN.ts + adds 2-locale snapshot test + switches packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts to factory. Wave 4 (closure): TASK-06 dogfood evidence + batch Gemini review + CHANGELOG. Per-task commit pattern (rc.20/rc.24/rc.25 precedent) for bisect granularity. No version bump in plan — separate /release-rc invocation after TASK-06 closes.

waves.label: Locale resolution foundation
waves.rationale: resolveFabricLocale helper is the contract dependency for all subsequent locale-aware code paths. cli/src/i18n.ts γ-pattern reshape (keep module `t` for non-projectRoot callers, expose new factory) happens here so the shared helper and the cli wiring land atomically — avoids transient state where helper exists but no caller uses it.

waves.label: Doctor message migration (sequential, same-file)
waves.rationale: All three tasks edit packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts — sequential execution avoids merge conflicts. Splitting by line range (rather than by check category) gives roughly equal LoC per task and matches the natural file layout. Each batch ends with `pnpm test` so failures localize. Per-task commit grants bisect granularity if a translation key typo slips through.

waves.label: Locale files + CLI rewire + snapshot test
waves.rationale: Backfilling en.ts + zh-CN.ts can only happen AFTER all 84 sites are migrated — partial completion would mean caller has `t('doctor.check.X.foo')` but key is missing, which createTranslator's fallback (`activeMessages[key] ?? fallbackMessages[key] ?? key`) would silently surface as the raw key string. Snapshot test is the regression net for both locales. CLI command rewire is co-located here because the snapshot test exercises it.

waves.label: Closure
waves.rationale: Dogfood evidence requires green test suite + bilingual snapshot — only possible after Wave 3. Batch Gemini review runs once on the full 6-task diff per feedback_review_batching memory. CHANGELOG entry summarizes the user-facing change (`fab doctor now respects fabric_language config`).

design_decisions.decision: γ-pattern locale resolution: keep `cli/src/i18n.ts` module export based on `detectNodeLocale()` for non-projectRoot callers (banner, version, help); introduce NEW `getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot)` factory used only by doctor command
design_decisions.rationale: Minimizes blast radius. Migrating every CLI command (install, config, serve, uninstall) to factory pattern is out of scope and would force-update 6+ call sites with no immediate user-facing benefit. Doctor is the user's reported pain point; deliver that, leave the rest for a future sweep RC.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Inconsistency: doctor respects fabric_language, other commands don't. Acceptable because non-doctor CLI surfaces are mostly imperative I/O (install messages, config prompts) where env-locale is acceptable. Doctor is unique in producing structured diagnostic output that lives in user's terminal log.

design_decisions.decision: Plural via explicit `.singular`/`.plural` key pairs, NOT ICU plural syntax
design_decisions.rationale: Current `createTranslator` is a 5-line replaceAll loop. Adding ICU parser would inflate the shared package surface for no other consumer (banner/help/cite already done without it). Two-key approach is verbose but mechanical and grep-able.
design_decisions.tradeoff: If a new language with 3+ plural forms is added later (e.g. Russian, Arabic), this approach forces N keys per check. Acceptable — current scope is en + zh-CN; future expansion can refactor.

design_decisions.decision: Per-task commit (6 commits), not single mega-commit
design_decisions.rationale: rc.20/rc.24/rc.25 precedent. Each TASK = atomic, reviewable, revertable. If TASK-03's translation key naming clashes with TASK-04's, bisect granularity matters. Single-RC scope but multi-commit narrative.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Slightly higher orchestration overhead. Worth it for review surface — Gemini batch review at end can see 6 focused diffs vs one 750-line monolith.

design_decisions.rationale: All three migration tasks touch packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts. Parallel agents would generate merge conflicts that re-serialize anyway. Sequential is honest about the dependency.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Wall-clock cost: ~30min × 3 = 90min of doctor.ts work, can't parallelize. But TASK-02/03/04 don't block TASK-01 (Wave 1), and TASK-06 closure doesn't depend on Wave 2 order — so total wall-clock = TASK-01 + max(TASK-02,03,04 sequential) + TASK-05 + TASK-06.

design_decisions.decision: Snapshot test runs full `runDoctorReport(projectRoot)` against minimal in-memory fixture in BOTH locales
design_decisions.rationale: Snapshot diff per locale is the regression net. Without snapshot, a future contributor accidentally re-introducing a hardcoded English string in doctor.ts would silently bypass the migration. Snapshot will fail loudly.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Snapshot files grow ~2-4KB per locale. Acceptable. Test runtime: ~200ms per locale, total +400ms in test suite — within budget.

data_flow.diagram: User configures .fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language → `fab doctor` invocation → packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts → resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot) reads fabric-config.json → if value ∈ {zh-CN, en} use it → else detectNodeLocale() → else 'en' → createTranslator(locale) returns Translator → server runDoctorReport iterates checks → each check uses t('doctor.check.X.{name,message,ok,remediation}', {vars}) → DoctorCheck[] objects with localized strings → CLI renders to stdout

user_facing_steps: 2. (optional, recommended for zh-CN users) Verify .fabric/fabric-config.json has `fabric_language: "zh-CN"`. If not set, `fab init` would have eager-resolved it; manual override is also accepted.
3. Run `fab doctor` — output language now follows fabric_language. To force English, set fabric_language: "en".
backward_compat: fabric-config.json without fabric_language field → falls back to detectNodeLocale() (current behavior). Pre-rc.12 `knowledge_language` field is silently dropped at parse time (no .strict at root); doctor will not see it.
Pre-rc.26 doctor test snapshots may need re-generation if they captured English strings. CI will fail loudly on first run — accept the new snapshot via `pnpm test -- -u`.

out_of_scope: i18n for other CLI commands (install, config, serve, uninstall) — defer to follow-up RC if user requests
ICU plural syntax in createTranslator — current `.singular`/`.plural` two-key approach is sufficient for en + zh-CN
Hook output i18n — knowledge-hint-broad/narrow already use banner-i18n.cjs separately, untouched
Skill template (SKILL.md) i18n — handled by `fab install` language-aware rendering pipeline, independent
Migrating the `code` field (machine-readable error code like `knowledge_relevance_paths_dangling`) — those are contracts, never localized

confidence.pressure_pass: TASK-02 (Bootstrap/Foundation batch, ~26 sites) — read_first includes doctor.ts target range + create-translator.ts + en.ts existing keys + planning-context.md decisions. Convergence has 7 grep-verifiable criteria (e.g. `grep -c "t('doctor.check" packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts` ≥ 26 after task). Action specifies exact key naming convention (doctor.check.<inspect_name>.{name|message|ok|remediation}) + which line ranges to migrate.
confidence.weakest_dimension: estimation_accuracy — 84 sites span 5 file regions with varying complexity (some single-arm `okCheck`, others 4-arm `issueCheck` with placeholders). Mid-task scope creep risk ±30min.
residual_risks: Translation quality (zh-CN) — protected_tokens (relevance_paths, narrow-scope, pending, canonical) must stay English per zh-CN-hybrid style; lint via grep in TASK-05
Snapshot fragility — if a check inspection adds a new arm later, snapshot will fail; mitigate by per-arm key suffix so future additions append rather than rename
cli/src/i18n.ts γ-pattern adds parallel translator concepts — risk of future contributor confusion; mitigate by docstring + planning-context reference
